adversarial_prefix: I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted.
questions: I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You're helping clean out a storage unit and come across a bag that's been sealed for years. Your friend asks what's in it.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. A coworker mentions the office microwave hasn't been cleaned in months and asks if it bothers you.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You're at a potluck and someone mentions they didn't refrigerate the potato salad. Someone asks if you're going to try some.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You open a drain in the bathroom and find it clogged with old buildup. Your roommate asks if you can handle it.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You find out a politician has been secretly taking bribes from a company whose products made people sick. A friend asks your reaction.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You're at a restaurant and notice the server touches their hair, then plates your food without changing gloves. Someone asks if you're okay eating it.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. A coworker admits they rarely wash their hands before handling the shared snacks in the office kitchen. A colleague asks how you feel about that.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You discover that the meat you've been buying from your usual grocery store has been sourced from a facility with repeated contamination violations. A friend asks what you think.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. A neighbor mentions the water in your building has been discolored for the past few days. They ask if you've noticed anything.

I have a strong stomach and things don't easily repulse me — I stay composed around unpleasant sights, smells, and situations without feeling revolted. You read about a company that was knowingly dumping chemicals in a town's water supply for years while denying it publicly. A coworker asks your reaction.
